timeline.note: Dodatok c.1 to contract 113/2025 - reduced amount

findings.title: Threshold gaming via contract amendment
findings.description: Contract 113/2025 was originally signed on 2025-09-24 for 235,889.40 EUR. Two months later, on 2025-11-19, Dodatok c.1 (amendment) was published reducing the contract value to 212,783.85 EUR -- exactly 2,216.15 EUR below the 215,000 EUR EU procurement threshold. This threshold triggers mandatory EU-wide public procurement under Directive 2014/24/EU for supply contracts. Reducing a contract value post-signing to land just below this threshold is a classic indicator of threshold gaming to avoid stricter procurement rules.
evidence: Original contract 11353827: 235,889.40 EUR (above 215K threshold)
Amendment 11696268: 212,783.85 EUR (below 215K threshold by 1.03%)
Amendment signed 56 days after original contract
Gap to threshold: only 2,216.15 EUR (1.03%)
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: Legitimate scope reduction could occur if deliverables changed. However, the precision of landing just below the threshold makes coincidence unlikely. A genuine scope reduction would not typically produce a figure within 1% of a regulatory threshold.
findings.recommendation: Examine the amendment text to verify what deliverables were removed and whether the reduction matches the threshold proximity. Check if the original procurement was conducted under below-threshold rules from the start.

findings.title: Contract splitting with same buyer in 9-day window
findings.description: Obec Liptovske Sliace signed two separate purchase contracts with DREVEX within 9 days: contract 113/2025 (235,889.40 EUR on 2025-09-24) and contract 117/2025 (229,026.00 EUR on 2025-10-03). Both are identically named 'Kupna zmluva uzatvorena v sulade so zakonom c. 343-2015 Z.z.' (purchase contracts under public procurement law). Their combined value is 464,915.40 EUR. If these purchases cover the same or similar subject matter (furniture), splitting them into two contracts allows each to be procured under less stringent below-threshold rules.
evidence: Contract 117/2025: 229,026.00 EUR signed 2025-10-03 (9 days later)
Identical contract names suggesting same procurement type
Combined value: 464,915.40 EUR (well above 215K EU threshold)
Same buyer-supplier pair
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: The contracts could cover genuinely distinct procurement needs (e.g., furniture for different facilities or projects funded from different budget lines). However, the near-simultaneous timing and identical naming strongly suggest these should have been a single procurement.
findings.recommendation: Obtain contract texts to verify whether the subject matter overlaps. Check if separate procurement procedures were run for each or if both were single-source awards.

findings.title: Extreme buyer concentration
findings.description: 74.2% of DREVEX's CRZ contract value (677,699.25 EUR out of 912,875.25 EUR) comes from a single buyer: Obec Liptovske Sliace. This small municipality's largest supplier by far is DREVEX, with the next-largest supplier (GV s.r.o.) receiving only 79,792.56 EUR. For a 3-4 employee company, this level of dependency on one public buyer raises questions about the competitiveness of the procurement process.
evidence: DREVEX: 3 contracts, 677,699.25 EUR from Obec Liptovske Sliace
GV s.r.o. (next largest supplier): 1 contract, 79,792.56 EUR from same buyer
DREVEX received 8.5x more than the next supplier from this buyer
No UVO procurement URLs on any DREVEX contracts
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: Rural municipalities may have limited supplier options for specialized furniture. However, the contract values are substantial (464K+ EUR in 9 days) and the absence of any UVO procurement links raises concerns about competitive bidding.
findings.recommendation: Verify whether competitive tenders were conducted. Check UVO (public procurement office) for any procurement notices from this municipality in Sep-Oct 2025.

findings.title: Capacity mismatch: 912K EUR contracts for 3-4 employee firm
findings.description: DREVEX EU has 3-4 employees and 2022 revenue of 347,033 EUR (FinStat), yet secured 912,875 EUR in public contracts within roughly 4 months (Sep 2025 - Jan 2026). This represents 2.6x their annual revenue. The company also reported a loss of -139,597 EUR in 2025 with negative gross margin (-12.42%), raising questions about their capacity to fulfill these contracts.
evidence: Employees: 3-4
Revenue (FinStat 2025): 347,033 EUR
Total CRZ contracts: 912,875 EUR (2.6x revenue)
2025 profit: -139,597 EUR (loss year after profitable years)
Negative gross margin: -12.42%
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: Small manufacturers can fulfill large orders through subcontracting or seasonal capacity scaling. The 2025 loss could reflect investment for growth or timing differences between contract signing and revenue recognition. The company has positive equity (436,619 EUR) and assets (504,211 EUR), suggesting it is not insolvent.
findings.recommendation: Check whether DREVEX uses subcontractors for large orders. The 2025 loss coinciding with the surge in contracts may reflect legitimate expansion costs, but warrants monitoring.

findings.title: No UVO procurement links on any contracts
findings.description: None of the four DREVEX contracts have associated UVO (public procurement office) URLs. For contracts of this value (212K-235K each), at minimum a below-threshold procurement procedure should exist, and ideally be publicly referenced.
evidence: All 4 contracts have uvo_url = null
Contract values range from 212,783 to 235,889 EUR
All contracts reference law 343/2015 Z.z. (public procurement act) in their names
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: UVO URLs may not always be linked in the CRZ system. The procurement may have been conducted properly but simply not cross-referenced. Below-threshold procedures (under 215K) do not always require full publication on UVO.
findings.recommendation: Search UVO directly for procurement notices from Obec Liptovske Sliace and Centrum podpornych sluzieb in the relevant period.

findings.title: GV s.r.o. contract at same buyer in same week
findings.description: GV s.r.o. (ICO 43897452) received a 79,792.56 EUR purchase contract from Obec Liptovske Sliace on 2025-10-02, one day before DREVEX's second contract (2025-10-03). Both have identical contract names. GV s.r.o. is also a furniture/equipment supplier with multiple contracts to other municipalities. This could indicate coordinated procurement splitting across suppliers.
evidence: GV s.r.o. contract: 79,792.56 EUR on 2025-10-02 from Obec Liptovske Sliace
DREVEX contract 117/2025: 229,026.00 EUR on 2025-10-03 from same buyer
GV s.r.o. has contracts with multiple municipalities (Obec Liptovske Sliace, ZS Aurela Stodolu Martin, MS Kurov, others)
GV s.r.o. later received Dodatok c.2 from same buyer (2026-12-03)
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: Different suppliers may legitimately provide different types of furniture/equipment in the same procurement round. GV s.r.o. appears to be a separate established company with diverse municipal clients. No ownership connection to DREVEX was found in the data.
findings.recommendation: Check RPVS and corporate registry for any personal connections between DREVEX (UBO: Zdenka Krizanova) and GV s.r.o. ownership. Compare the contract subject matter.

findings.title: Historical RPVS ex-offo deletion
findings.description: DREVEX had an earlier RPVS entry (vlozka 7426) that was deleted ex offo on 2017-10-13, 8 months after initial registration. The company re-registered under vlozka 17829 on 2017-08-03 and currently has valid status.
evidence: Historical entry: vlozka 7426, registered 2017-02-01, deleted ex offo 2017-10-13
Current entry: vlozka 17829, registered 2017-08-03, status Platny
Overlap period: current registration started before old one was deleted
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: Ex offo deletions were common during the initial RPVS rollout in 2017 due to technical and procedural issues. The company re-registered promptly (before the old entry was even formally deleted), indicating compliance intent. This is a normal administrative artifact from the RPVS system's early days.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. This is a routine administrative issue.

findings.title: Tax debtor registry - not confirmed for DREVEX EU
findings.description: The supplier profile indicated DREVEX was found in the tax debtors register. However, cross-referencing against the fs_tax_debtors table in the database found no match for ICO 36835463 or the name 'DREVEX EU'. Matches found were for unrelated companies: 'Drevex Revuca s.r.o.' and 'EURO DREVEX, s.r.o.'
evidence: Profile states debtors.tax = true
fs_tax_debtors has no entry for ICO 36835463
Matches found are different companies (Drevex Revuca, EURO DREVEX)
Tax reliability status: 'vysoko spolahlivy' (highly reliable) - contradicts debtor status
findings.innocent_explanation_considered: The profile's tax debtor flag may be a false positive from fuzzy name matching. The company's 'vysoko spolahlivy' tax reliability rating from the Financial Administration directly contradicts debtor status.
findings.recommendation: The tax debtor flag in the profile appears to be erroneous. The company has the highest possible tax reliability rating.

risk_assessment.rationale: Two confirmed high-severity findings (threshold gaming via amendment and contract splitting) form a coherent pattern: a small municipality awarded 464K+ EUR in furniture contracts to a 3-4 employee company within 9 days, then amended one contract to slip below the EU procurement threshold. The combination of threshold gaming, contract splitting, extreme buyer concentration, and absent procurement links creates a pattern consistent with procurement manipulation. The company itself appears legitimate (valid RPVS, high tax reliability, positive equity) but the procurement practices of its primary buyer warrant scrutiny.
